title: Global Stylesheet
content:
This file defines the global CSS variables, animations, and base styles for the DocMed application, leveraging Tailwind CSS. ### Purpose - **Theme Definition:** Establishes a consistent color palette, typography, and spacing using CSS custom properties (variables). - **Utility Classes:** Provides base styles for common HTML elements and custom classes for specific UI components. - **Animations:** Defines keyframe animations for visual effects like ripples and orbits. ### Key Features - **CSS Custom Properties:** A comprehensive set of `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius`, and animation-related variables are defined in the `:root` scope. - **Tailwind CSS Integration:** Imports Tailwind CSS using `@import "tailwindcss";` to enable utility-first styling. - **Custom Animations:** Implements `@keyframes ripple` and `@keyframes orbit` for interactive and dynamic elements. - **Base Styles:** Sets `box-sizing`, `scroll-behavior`, and base `body` styles for consistent rendering. - **Component Styles:** Includes styles for specific elements like `.g-button` and `.styled-scrollbar`. ### Usage This file is imported at the top level of the application (likely in `app/layout.tsx` or a similar entry point) to ensure all styles are globally available. Developers can then utilize the defined CSS variables directly in their components or leverage Tailwind CSS classes that are configured to use these variables. **Example of using a CSS variable:** ```css .my-element { background-color: hsl(var(--background)); color: hsl(var(--foreground)); } ``` **Example of using a custom class:** ```html ``` **Example of applying scrollbar styles:** ```html
```
